hero.title: 6 Days in Manali.
hero.titleAccent: Snow Peaks, Apple Orchards and the Beas River.
hero.intro: Manali is the Himalayas at their most welcoming. Snow-capped peaks, the rushing Beas river, apple orchards, hot springs and the high Rohtang and Atal Tunnel adventures. Six days in one of India's favourite mountain towns.
hero.heroImageAlt: Manali snow peaks Beas river Himachal

overview.heading: 6 Days Manali: The Full Picture
body: Manali, in the Kullu Valley of Himachal Pradesh, is one of India's most loved mountain destinations. Set on the banks of the Beas river beneath snow-capped peaks, it offers temples, hot springs, apple orchards, adventure sports and easy access to high-altitude wonders like Solang Valley and, in season, the Rohtang Pass and Atal Tunnel.

This 6 day Manali package from Malaysia is private throughout, with your own vehicle and driver guide. There is time for the old town and Hadimba Temple, the snow and activities of Solang Valley, the Vashisht hot springs, and the dramatic mountain drives, all at a relaxed pace in the cool Himalayan air.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Solang Valley
items.body: A stunning valley near Manali, famous for snow in winter and paragliding, zorbing and cable-car rides in summer. The mountain scenery is spectacular year-round.

items.title: Old Manali & Hadimba Temple
items.body: The charming old town, the ancient cedar-shaded Hadimba Devi Temple, and the cafes and lanes that give Manali its relaxed, bohemian character.

items.title: Rohtang Pass & Atal Tunnel
items.body: In season, the dramatic Rohtang Pass and the engineering marvel of the Atal Tunnel open up high Himalayan landscapes of snow and stark beauty.

days.title: Arrive & Transfer to Manali
days.description: Met at the airport (often Chandigarh) and driven up to Manali, or fly to Kullu. Evening at leisure.

days.title: Manali Local Sightseeing
days.description: The old town, temples and hot springs.

days.title: Solang Valley
days.description: A full day at Solang Valley for scenery and activities.

days.title: Rohtang / Atal Tunnel Excursion
days.description: A high-altitude excursion (season and permits permitting).

days.title: Kullu Valley & Leisure
days.description: Kullu valley sights, river activities and shopping.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Manali's Mountains, Handled With Care
whyBook.body: High passes, permits and mountain roads make Manali a trip that rewards local expertise. We handle the drives, the permits and the timings. Dhesu has run North India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Manali Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
Manali is one of India's most loved Himalayan destinations, and this 6 day package covers it at a relaxed pace. Snow peaks, the Beas river, Solang Valley, hot springs and high passes give a full mountain experience.

A private vehicle and driver guide handle the mountain roads, permits and timings, so you can focus on the scenery and the activities.

For more of the hills, combine with Shimla in our 9 day Shimla and Manali package.

sections.heading: Rohtang Pass and the Atal Tunnel
sections.body:
The Rohtang Pass, at over 3,900 metres, offers dramatic high-Himalayan scenery and snow, though access depends on the season and permits. The Atal Tunnel, one of the longest highway tunnels in the world, has made the Lahaul valley accessible year-round.

We arrange these excursions where conditions allow, and advise on the best options for the season. They are among the most memorable parts of a Manali trip.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Kullu-Manali or Chandigarh Airport (KUU / IXC). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Manali?
items.answer: March to June is pleasant and popular, with snow lingering at higher points. December to February brings full snow, beautiful but cold. The monsoon (July to September) is lush but can cause landslides. October to November offers crisp, clear mountain views.

items.question: Is the Rohtang Pass always accessible?
items.answer: No. Rohtang Pass is seasonal and requires permits, and is usually open from around May to October, weather permitting. The Atal Tunnel provides year-round access to the Lahaul valley. We arrange the best available high-altitude excursion for your dates.

items.question: How do I reach Manali?
items.answer: Most travellers fly to Chandigarh or Kullu and drive up to Manali, a scenic mountain journey. We arrange the transfers as part of the package. The drive itself, along the Beas river, is part of the experience.
